metadata.title: dfetch CRA Compliance Component Definition

roles.title: Software Supplier
roles.description: Organisation that develops and distributes dfetch.

roles.title: Maintainer
roles.description: Primary maintainer of the dfetch project and this compliance document.

parties.name: dfetch-org
parties.remarks: Non-commercial open-source organisation. dfetch is not placed on the market in the context of a commercial activity.

metadata.remarks: Produced voluntarily under Article 13(5) to support downstream integrators. dfetch is non-commercial open-source software and is not subject to mandatory CRA conformity obligations (Recital 18).

components.title: dfetch
components.description: Python CLI tool for vendoring source-code dependencies from Git, SVN, or archive files into a project as plain files.
components.purpose: Vendor source-code dependencies from Git, SVN, or archive files into a project as plain files, enabling reproducible builds without submodules or externals. Supports integrity hashing, plaintext-transport detection, and credential redaction to reduce supply-chain risk.

control-implementations.description: Voluntary alignment with prEN 40000-1-4 Security Objectives. The catalog organises CRA Annex I Part I essential requirements (ECR-a–m) as groups; this component implements the Security Objectives (SO.*) within each ECR using dfetch controls (C-001–C-046).

statements.description: GEC-1: C-015 (CodeQL), C-016 (dependency-review), C-017 (bandit), C-022 (SBOM) address known-vulnerability detection in CI. C-043 (pip-audit OSV gate) blocks release if runtime dependencies carry known vulnerabilities.

links.text: C-022 SBOM generation; C-043 pip-audit CVE gate

props.name: not-applicable
props.value: GEC-2, GEC-3, GEC-4, GEC-5 (no exposed network services — CLI tool); GEC-7 (no external sensing capabilities); AUM-5 (no password or authentication mechanism)

props.name: gaps
props.value: Integrity hash verification (:ref:`C-005 <c-005>`) is opt-in; manifest entries without an ``integrity`` field are fetched without hash verification by default

statements.description: GEC-12 (no unneeded software components): C-001 enforces minimal runtime dependencies. dfetch does not expose network services. Gaps: Integrity hash verification (:ref:`C-005 <c-005>`) is opt-in; manifest entries without an ``integrity`` field are fetched without hash verification by default Not applicable: GEC-2, GEC-3, GEC-4, GEC-5 (no exposed network services — CLI tool); GEC-7 (no external sensing capabilities); AUM-5 (no password or authentication mechanism)

links.text: C-002 Decompression-bomb protection; C-003 Symlink validation; C-004 Member-type checks

props.name: not-applicable
props.value: GEC-9 (no security-relevant startup configuration state in dfetch)

statements.description: dfetch reads only its manifest at startup; no security-sensitive config initialisation. Not applicable: GEC-9 (no security-relevant startup configuration state in dfetch)

props.name: not-applicable
props.value: DLM-1-b, GEC-10 (no persistent device state requiring factory reset — CLI tool)

statements.description: dfetch is a stateless CLI tool; no factory-reset concept applies. Not applicable: DLM-1-b, GEC-10 (no persistent device state requiring factory reset — CLI tool)

SUM-1/SUM-2: Updates distributed via PyPI (``pip install --upgrade dfetch``) and GitHub Releases. pip's TLS-protected download and version-pinning model satisfies SUM-2. No dfetch-specific update mechanism is needed or implemented; the package manager is the update vehicle.

props.name: not-applicable
props.value: SUM-3 (automatic updates managed by pip/pipenv/poetry — not dfetch itself)

statements.description: Update automation is the responsibility of the user's package manager. Not applicable: SUM-3 (automatic updates managed by pip/pipenv/poetry — not dfetch itself)

UNM-4: ``dfetch check`` and ``dfetch environment`` both call ``newer_version_available()`` (``dfetch/util/github_version_check.py``), which polls the GitHub releases API and prints a notice if a newer dfetch release exists.

links.text: Version check in dfetch check (suppressed in CI)

props.name: not-applicable
props.value: SUM-4 (update scheduling controlled by pip/pipenv — not dfetch)

statements.description: Postponement is handled by the user's package manager. Not applicable: SUM-4 (update scheduling controlled by pip/pipenv — not dfetch)

props.name: not-applicable
props.value: ACM-2, AUM-2, AUM-3, AUM-4, AUM-6 (dfetch has no user-facing authentication or access control)

props.name: gaps
props.value: dfetch has no native authentication or authorisation layer; access control is fully delegated to the underlying VCS server and host OS. C-006 prevents interactive credential prompts, and C-036 strips credentials from persisted metadata — both are confidentiality controls, not access-control mechanisms in the authentication/authorisation sense

statements.description: dfetch delegates authentication to the host VCS client (git, svn) and the OS credential store. C-006 prevents SSH command injection; C-036 strips credentials from stored metadata. Gaps: dfetch has no native authentication or authorisation layer; access control is fully delegated to the underlying VCS server and host OS. C-006 prevents interactive credential prompts, and C-036 strips credentials from persisted metadata — both are confidentiality controls, not access-control mechanisms in the authentication/authorisation sense Not applicable: ACM-2, AUM-2, AUM-3, AUM-4, AUM-6 (dfetch has no user-facing authentication or access control)

links.text: C-036 Credential redaction from stored metadata

props.name: gaps
props.value: No persistent log of unauthorised access attempts

statements.description: GEC-13: C-045 (plaintext transport warning) alerts on unauthenticated connections. No persistent security event log. Gaps: No persistent log of unauthorised access attempts

SSM-1/SSM-3: The developer workstation is a trusted boundary (as established in the usage threat model: 'Trusted at workstation invocation time'). C-036 strips all credentials from .dfetch_data.yaml before write, so the stored file contains only non-sensitive metadata (remote URL without userinfo, revision, content hash, timestamp). The threat model explicitly marks metadata_store.storesSensitiveData = False and isDestEncryptedAtRest = False as a conscious design choice; no encryption-at-rest is required.
GEC-8: C-005 (constant-time comparison), C-034 (temp-file cleanup) protect in-process data.

links.text: C-005 Constant-time HMAC comparison; C-034 SHA-256/384/512 allowlist

props.name: gaps
props.value: C-045 warns on plaintext-scheme URLs but does not refuse to proceed; TLS/SSH confidentiality is provided by the underlying VCS client, not enforced by dfetch itself

statements.description: SCM-3/SCM-4: Plaintext transport (http://, git://, svn://) is accepted by design for legacy source compatibility. C-045 detects and warns the user before proceeding — 'Detection only; dfetch still proceeds with the plaintext connection; the control raises user awareness but does not enforce scheme selection' (usage threat model, C-045 description). For archive URLs over HTTP, C-005 (integrity hash) verifies that content has not been tampered with in transit; it does not encrypt or conceal the content. This is a deliberate design decision, not a residual gap. Gaps: C-045 warns on plaintext-scheme URLs but does not refuse to proceed; TLS/SSH confidentiality is provided by the underlying VCS client, not enforced by dfetch itself

props.name: gaps
props.value: Server authentication (TLS certificate verification, SSH host-key checking) is delegated to the OS trust store and VCS client; dfetch does not independently authenticate remote endpoints and cannot enforce authenticated channels when C-045's warning is overridden by the user

statements.description: SCM-2: HTTPS connections authenticate via TLS CA chain (C-003); SSH connections authenticate via host-key verification (C-004). Plain git:// and svn:// connections lack channel-level authentication but are accepted by design for legacy compatibility — the same rationale as DataTransmittedConfidentiality — and C-045 warns the user. The usage threat model notes the network adversary 'cannot break correctly implemented TLS or SSH'; the residual risk for unauthenticated transports is an accepted design trade-off. Gaps: Server authentication (TLS certificate verification, SSH host-key checking) is delegated to the OS trust store and VCS client; dfetch does not independently authenticate remote endpoints and cannot enforce authenticated channels when C-045's warning is overridden by the user

links.text: C-003 Archive symlink validation; C-004 Archive member-type checks

props.name: not-applicable
props.value: CCK-1, CCK-2, CCK-3 (dfetch manages no cryptographic keys)

statements.description: CRY-1: C-005 uses Python's hashlib with SHA-256 for integrity hashes. No key management is required or performed by dfetch. Not applicable: CCK-1, CCK-2, CCK-3 (dfetch manages no cryptographic keys)

props.name: gaps
props.value: Integrity hash opt-in only; not enforced by default for git/svn

statements.description: SSM-2: C-005 (integrity hash in .dfetch_data.yaml) provides optional stored-data integrity verification. Gaps: Integrity hash opt-in only; not enforced by default for git/svn
GEC-8: C-005 and C-034 protect data integrity during processing.

links.text: C-005 Integrity hash; C-034 SHA-256/384/512 allowlist

props.name: gaps
props.value: C-005 provides end-to-end hash verification for archive sources only (opt-in); git and svn sources rely solely on VCS object integrity (SHA-1/SHA-256 object model) and TLS/SSH channel integrity — no dfetch-level hash verification

statements.description: SCM-2: TLS (C-003) and SSH (C-004) provide channel-level integrity. Commit-hash pinning (rev: <sha>) provides content-level integrity for git. Gaps: C-005 provides end-to-end hash verification for archive sources only (opt-in); git and svn sources rely solely on VCS object integrity (SHA-1/SHA-256 object model) and TLS/SSH channel integrity — no dfetch-level hash verification

links.text: C-003 Archive symlink validation; C-004 Archive member-type checks

statements.description: GEC-13-f: dfetch surfaces transport-integrity warnings (C-045) at runtime but does not maintain a persistent security event log. Gaps: No persistent integrity-violation log

links.text: C-045 Plaintext transport warning at runtime

props.name: not-applicable
props.value: UNM-1, UNM-2 (dfetch processes no personal data requiring user notification); DTM-3 (no optional data processing to configure)

statements.description: DTM-1: C-044 documents that .dfetch_data.yaml is limited to remote_url (stripped), revision, optional hash, and last_fetch — each justified by functional necessity. DTM-2: met by design — dfetch collects no telemetry or optional data. Not applicable: UNM-1, UNM-2 (dfetch processes no personal data requiring user notification); DTM-3 (no optional data processing to configure)

links.text: Metadata model — only non-sensitive fields stored

props.name: not-applicable
props.value: RLM-2, RLM-6 (CLI tool; no persistent device state or control-system backup needed)

statements.description: dfetch is a stateless CLI tool. Recovery consists of re-running dfetch update, which re-fetches all dependencies. Not applicable: RLM-2, RLM-6 (CLI tool; no persistent device state or control-system backup needed)

props.name: not-applicable
props.value: RLM-3, RLM-4, RLM-5 (no persistent network services to prioritize)

props.name: gaps
props.value: No timeout on VCS operations (potential resource exhaustion)

statements.description: RLM-1: C-002 (no background daemon) and C-007 (subprocess controls) reduce exposure. DoS resilience applies only to the transient fetch operation. Gaps: No timeout on VCS operations (potential resource exhaustion) Not applicable: RLM-3, RLM-4, RLM-5 (no persistent network services to prioritize)

props.name: not-applicable
props.value: TCM-1 (dfetch makes targeted VCS fetch requests; no ambient outbound traffic to throttle)

props.name: gaps
props.value: Archive HTTP operations time out at 15 s (reachability) and 60 s (download) via ``archive.py``; git and svn subprocess calls have no timeout and can stall indefinitely

statements.description: GEC-8-i: C-001 (minimal deps) and C-007 (subprocess controls) reduce the risk of dfetch being weaponised against external services. LIM-1: dfetch fetches only what is listed in the manifest. Gaps: Archive HTTP operations time out at 15 s (reachability) and 60 s (download) via ``archive.py``; git and svn subprocess calls have no timeout and can stall indefinitely Not applicable: TCM-1 (dfetch makes targeted VCS fetch requests; no ambient outbound traffic to throttle)

LIM-2: Path traversal to destinations outside the project tree is prevented by C-001 (check_no_path_traversal() via realpath). The residual risk of a manifest declaring a legitimate but sensitive dst: (e.g. .github/workflows/) is accepted in the usage threat model under the 'Manifest under code review' assumption: dfetch.yaml is version-controlled and any such dst: change would be rejected at review.

links.text: C-008 Manifest URL and path validation

props.name: not-applicable
props.value: NMM-1 (dfetch makes no ambient outbound network traffic to monitor)

statements.description: dfetch makes targeted, user-initiated VCS requests only. Not applicable: NMM-1 (dfetch makes no ambient outbound network traffic to monitor)

props.name: not-applicable
props.value: GEC-2-j, GEC-3-j, GEC-4-j, GEC-5-j, GEC-7-j (dfetch exposes no network services)

props.name: gaps
props.value: No domain or URL-scheme allowlist constrains which remote URLs the manifest may reference; git and svn subprocess calls have no timeout (archive HTTP operations time out at 15 s / 60 s)

statements.description: GEC-6: C-007 (no shell=True), C-008 (URL/path validation) implement input validation. GEC-12-j: C-001 enforces minimal runtime dependencies. Gaps: No domain or URL-scheme allowlist constrains which remote URLs the manifest may reference; git and svn subprocess calls have no timeout (archive HTTP operations time out at 15 s / 60 s) Not applicable: GEC-2-j, GEC-3-j, GEC-4-j, GEC-5-j, GEC-7-j (dfetch exposes no network services)

links.text: C-003 Symlink validation; C-004 Member-type checks

props.name: not-applicable
props.value: Compile-time mitigations (CFI, sandboxing) — not applicable to pure Python

statements.description: GEC-11: Python interpreter provides ASLR/DEP/stack-canaries (OS-level). dfetch: no eval/exec of remote content; constant-time comparison (C-005); shell=False (C-007); static analysis (C-015, C-017). C-046 formalises this inventory in doc/explanation/compliance_track.rst. Not applicable: Compile-time mitigations (CFI, sandboxing) — not applicable to pure Python

links.text: C-007 No shell=True in subprocess calls

props.name: gaps
props.value: No persistent structured security event log (LGM-1/2/3/4 gap). dfetch prints operational output to stderr but does not retain it, does not record which credentials were used, which files were modified, or when remote access occurred. C-036 ensures credentials are excluded from operational output but is not a logging control

statements.description: LGM-1: dfetch logs warnings to stderr during a run but does not persist them. LGM-6: C-036 ensures credentials are not logged. Gaps: No persistent structured security event log (LGM-1/2/3/4 gap). dfetch prints operational output to stderr but does not retain it, does not record which credentials were used, which files were modified, or when remote access occurred. C-036 ensures credentials are excluded from operational output but is not a logging control

links.text: C-036 Credential exclusion from stored metadata and logs

statements.description: MON-1: C-045 (plaintext transport detection) monitors for insecure connections at runtime and surfaces warnings to the user. Not applicable: NMM-1 (no ambient network monitoring)

props.name: not-applicable
props.value: LGM-5 (dfetch does not persist logs; nothing to disable)

statements.description: dfetch emits transient stderr warnings only; no persistent log to opt out of. Not applicable: LGM-5 (dfetch does not persist logs; nothing to disable)
dfetch performs no ongoing monitoring between invocations. Not applicable: MON-2 (no persistent monitoring to disable)

props.name: not-applicable
props.value: No dfetch-specific secure-deletion control is required: dfetch stores no personal data and no keying material. The only on-disk state is .dfetch_data.yaml (non-sensitive metadata) and vendored source files (third-party code, not user data). Standard OS file deletion (rm / del) is sufficient; cryptographic wipe is not warranted. DLM-1 is satisfied by design (no sensitive data to wipe), not by a dedicated dfetch control.

statements.description: DLM-1: .dfetch_data.yaml contains only non-sensitive metadata — remote URL (credentials stripped by C-036), revision, optional content hash, and last-fetch timestamp. Standard OS file deletion is sufficient; no secure-wipe is required. Users delete the file and vendored directories to remove all dfetch data. ECR-m is satisfied by design because dfetch collects no personal data, credentials, or keying material on disk. Not applicable: No dfetch-specific secure-deletion control is required: dfetch stores no personal data and no keying material. The only on-disk state is .dfetch_data.yaml (non-sensitive metadata) and vendored source files (third-party code, not user data). Standard OS file deletion (rm / del) is sufficient; cryptographic wipe is not warranted. DLM-1 is satisfied by design (no sensitive data to wipe), not by a dedicated dfetch control.

props.name: not-applicable
props.value: DLM-2, DLM-3, DLM-4 (dfetch does not export user data to external systems)

statements.description: dfetch does not provide data export or transfer functionality. Not applicable: DLM-2, DLM-3, DLM-4 (dfetch does not export user data to external systems)
Not applicable — see SO.DataTransmittedConfidentiality (data export context). Not applicable: DLM-3 (no data export)
Not applicable — see SO.DataTransmittedConfidentiality (data export context). Not applicable: DLM-4 (no data export)
